etymology.description: Depicts a sharp peg -> originally referred to pegs used in board games -> conceptually extended to 'talent' as skill pegs straight into a task
etymology.interpretation: The character 才 (cái) initially depicted a sharp peg, which was associated with playing board games that required skill. This sense of skill was then extended metaphorically to mean 'talent'. The leap to 'only then' likely comes from the notion that one's capabilities are unlocked or recognized through achievement, akin to placing the right peg in the right spot.

examples.chinese: 他的音乐才华令人惊叹(他的音樂才華令人驚嘆)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yīnyuè cáihuá lìngrén jīngtàn.

examples.chinese: 我刚刚才知道这个消息(我剛剛才知道這個消息)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ gānggāng cái zhīdào zhège xiāoxi.
examples.english: I just now found out about this news.

examples.chinese: 只有努力工作才能取得成功(只有努力工作才能取得成功)。
examples.pinyin: Zhǐyǒu nǔlì gōngzuò cái néng qǔdé chénggōng.
examples.english: Only by working hard can one achieve success.
